q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is logger.warning, which is not network I/O, shell execution, DB write, or RCE.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: Log injection occurs at the logger.warning call; the function returns a JsonResponse and the injected newlines in the log are not observable from the return value or in-memory state after return.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: Not evaluated; decision stopped at Q2.

mock_guidance.target: Mock or patch the logger.warning call on line 73 to intercept the log message before it is written.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the full string argument passed to logger.warning, including any embedded newline (\n, \r) or control characters originating from request.body and the Authorization header.
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Prevent actual log output; simply record the arguments and allow execution to continue normally so the JsonResponse is still returned.
notes: The test should supply request.body or authorization header values containing newline characters to trigger the vulnerability.
Assert that the captured log message string contains the raw newline characters, proving lack of sanitization.
